q: What type of current is supplied to a house?
Which wire in the thick cable supplied to a house carries a high voltage and is considered dangerous?
What is the typical voltage of the neutral wire (N) in house wiring?
In what sequence is electricity fed into a house?
a: Meter, main switch, main fuse, distribution box.
Main fuse, meter, main switch, distribution box.
Distribution box, main switch, meter, main fuse.
Main switch, distribution box, main fuse, meter.

q: Which of the following is NOT one of the normal circuits from the distribution box in a house?
What is the new colour code for the live wire (L) in a wiring system?
What is the new colour code for the neutral wire (N) in a wiring system?
What is the old colour code for the live wire?
How are light bulbs typically arranged in a lighting circuit in a house?
What is the advantage of connecting light bulbs in parallel in a lighting circuit?
a: It doubles the voltage across each bulb.
Other bulbs are not affected when one bulb is switched ON or OFF.

q: What happens if bulbs in a lighting circuit are connected in series and one bulb is switched OFF?
a: Only that bulb's section turns OFF.
All other bulbs also switch OFF.

q: What two wires are found in a lighting circuit?
In a lighting circuit, what is the correct sequential connection?
a: Neutral wire, load, switch, fuse, live wire.
Live wire, fuse, switch, load, neutral wire.
Load, fuse, switch, live wire, neutral wire.
Switch, fuse, live wire, load, neutral wire.

q: What type of circuit connects distribution points to the main supply in a continuous closed circuit for power sockets?
What is the purpose of the earth wire (E) in a ring main circuit?
a: To provide a return path for current.

q: What is the fuse rating for each main ring circuit?
Which types of appliances are typically connected to heating circuits?
What is the fuse rating for heating circuits used with high power cookers?
Where must the fuse of each circuit be connected?
Why is a fuse connected in the neutral lead considered pointless?
a: It would cause a short circuit.
It will in no way protect the appliances used in the circuit.

q: What is an automatic switch that trips (turns OFF) when the current rises above a specified value?
What is considered the most important safety precaution in an electrical appliance?
a: Using fuses in the neutral wire.

q: How is the earth wire always connected in any electrical appliance?
Why is proper earthing crucial for safety if the live wire touches the metal case of an appliance?
a: It prevents the appliance from working.
It ensures the current passes through the person touching it.
It directs the current directly into the earth, sparing the person.
It increases the resistance of the human body.

q: The current enters the house through one wire and returns to the local substation through the other. Which wire carries high voltage?

a: To act as the main switch for the entire house.
To branch wires into several parallel circuits.
To connect to the local substation.
